feedback.text: I wish you a better day for the Commission of the European Union or for those who read it. I would like to ask you to expand this initiative by protecting video games already sold to consumers. Over the last decade, video game publishers tend to make the video games they have already sold unusable in different ways (by switching off server-dependent games servers, destroying patches, etc.), thereby almost stealing the customer. Although all kinds of objections were raised to explain this anti-consumer behaviour, the ultimate aim is clearly to introduce the envisaged limitation period in the toy industry, a best example of which is the message when Overwatch was shut down: “Even the best journeys have to end, but a new one is around the corner. Thank you Heroes! See you October 4th" ---> Kontextus: On 4 October, Overwatch started 2. Blizzard (publisher) simply captured the first game and left the first game unplayed to buy the second. As a community with the most robust consumer protection apparatus in the world, the European Union should not be a platform for such anti-consumer market behaviour. I would like to ask you to include a section protecting video games in this Digital Fairness Act so that publishers cannot unilaterally buy for what you have already paid for. Thank you!
